name: Cable Crossover
resistance_profile: Constant cable tension through adduction; set pulley height to bias upper/lower chest.
execution_steps: Set both pulleys high and select a light-to-moderate weight, then grab a handle in each hand.
Step forward into a staggered stance with a slight forward lean and a soft, fixed bend in your elbows.
Open your arms out to a comfortable chest stretch, keeping the elbows bent and steady.
Sweep your hands down and together in front of you and squeeze the chest hard at the finish.
Control the handles back out to the stretch and repeat.
good_when: You want constant tension and a strong peak-contraction squeeze on the chest.
You are adding isolation volume after your main presses.
You want to bias upper or lower chest by changing pulley height.
bad_when: You want to build maximal pressing strength or load heavy (use a barbell or dumbbell press).
